title: proposal(dockerfile/parse): vendor moby/buildkit instead of docker/docker
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**



/kind feature

**Description**

I was trying to vendor `github.com/containers/libpod` in my project with `go mod vendor`, but it keeps failing on finding `github.com/docker/docker/builder/dockerfile/parser`, which has been removed from `github.com/docker/docker` since [moby/moby c9ebd2f13b3aab49c2d90cc38dcf2cd0d059697f](https://github.com/moby/moby/commit/c9ebd2f13b3aab49c2d90cc38dcf2cd0d059697f).

Though I can pin `docker/docker` to `v17.03.2-ce` as a work around, but `github.com/Sirupsen/logrus` will cause
```
case-insensitive import collision: "github.com/Sirupsen/logrus" and "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
```

And I have tried `replace github.com/Sirupsen/logrus v1.4.0 => github.com/sirupsen/logrus v1.4.0` but with no luck :/

So, is it possible to change the current `github.com/docker/docker/builder/dockerfile/parser` dependency to `github.com/moby/buildkit/frontend/dockerfile/parser` which could fix this problem once for all?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a go project using `go mod init`

2. import and use `github.com/containers/libpod/libpod`

3. vendor dependencies using `go mod vendor`

**Describe the results you received:**

fail to vendor `github.com/containers/libpod/libpod` at finding `github.com/docker/docker/builder/dockerfile/parser`

**Describe the results you expected:**

vendor `github.com/containers/libpod/libpod` without any error

comments.body:
I just skimmed the issue:
`github.com/docker/docker/builder/dockerfile/parser` will be dropped when we vendor the latest buildah (Cc @TomSweeneyRedHat).

Regarding the usage of go mod:
We started working on making the dependencies `go mod`-friendly but I guess this will take some more time until we have ironed out the rough edges.

Thank you @vrothberg for the quick and informative reply,

Wow, really good news, happy to see `buildah` to be there. Anything I can help?
Best to help out by testing and reviewing patches.

Well, I have found a workaround to this issue with `replace`

```go
replace github.com/docker/docker/builder/dockerfile/parser v1.13.1 => github.com/moby/buildkit v0.4.0
```

where `v1.13.1` should be replaced with the same version name of `github.com/docker/docker` in you `require` block

> Best to help out by testing and reviewing patches.

@rhatdan , I will try, but honestly I have never reviewed any code from others.
@jeffreystoke No problem even reviewing docs or just trying out the code helps.
@rhatdan, this should be fixed now. Imagebuildah effectively forked the package of a later version, and everything has been properly propagated through the projects. Thanks for catching up on this!
